concept: sum the first size-k window
cue: Walk the loop and add up the first k elements.

concept: slide = add right, remove left
cue: Start from the first window's sum, then add the entering element and subtract the leaving one.
note: Reusing the running sum (add the entering value, subtract the leaving one) makes the whole scan O(n); re-adding all k elements per window is O(nk). Big-O just names how the work grows with the input size n: O(n) means the work grows in step with n, O(nk) means about n times k steps.

concept: max sum over every size-k block
cue: Slide the window across, keeping the largest running sum seen.

concept: max sum over size-k window
cue: Add up the first window's sum, then slide it and keep the max.
prompt: Write `maxSumK(nums, k)` that returns the largest sum of any contiguous block of exactly `k` elements. Slide a running window sum; do not recompute each block from scratch.
note: Recomputing every block from scratch is O(nk); the slide keeps it O(n) by carrying the sum forward.

concept: max average of size-k window
cue: Track the best window sum, then divide once at the end.
prompt: Write `maxAverageK(nums, k)` that returns the largest average of any contiguous block of exactly `k` elements. Maintain a running window sum and divide only at the end.

concept: count size-k blocks meeting a threshold
cue: Slide the window and tally each block whose sum clears the threshold.

concept: count windows at or above a threshold
cue: Slide a running sum and count the windows that meet the threshold.
prompt: Write `countWindowsAtLeast(nums, k, threshold)` that returns how many contiguous blocks of exactly `k` elements have a sum greater than or equal to `threshold`. Slide the window; do not recompute each block.

concept: count-map: increment on enter
cue: For each value, bump its tally in the map.
note: `m.get(x) || 0` reads x's current count, or 0 when x is not in the map yet: get returns undefined for a missing key, and `undefined || 0` gives 0.

concept: count-map slide with delete-on-zero
cue: Decrement each leaving key, and remove it once its count reaches zero.
note: Deleting a key the moment its count hits 0 keeps map.size equal to the number of distinct values actually present; leftover zero-count keys would over-count distinctness.

concept: shrink the left edge until the window is valid
cue: Drop elements from the left, one at a time, until the sum is no longer over target.
note: This is the shrink step of a variable-size window: when the window's sum grows too big, remove from the left until it fits again.

concept: grow-right, shrink-left while invalid
cue: Extend the right edge, shrink from the left while the sum is too big, track the widest span.

concept: longest subarray with sum at most target
cue: Expand right; whenever the sum exceeds target, shrink from the left.
prompt: Write `longestAtMost(nums, target)` that returns the length of the longest contiguous subarray whose sum is at most `target`. All values in `nums` are non-negative. Use a sliding window.

concept: shortest covering: measure inside the shrink
cue: Grow right; while the sum reaches target, record the width before dropping the left element.

concept: shortest subarray with sum at least target
cue: Expand right; while the window covers target, record its length and shrink left.
prompt: Write `minSubArrayLen(target, nums)` that returns the length of the shortest contiguous subarray whose sum is at least `target`, or `0` if none exists. All values in `nums` are non-negative. Use a sliding window.

concept: longest run with no repeated char
cue: Extend right; when the new char repeats, shrink left until it is unique again.

concept: length of longest substring without repeating chars
cue: Slide a window, shrinking left whenever the entering char duplicates.
prompt: Write `lengthOfLongestSubstring(s)` that returns the length of the longest substring of `s` with no repeated character. Use a sliding window.
mapsTo: LC 3 · Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters

concept: longest window with at most k distinct
cue: Grow right; while the map holds more than k keys, shrink left and drop emptied keys.

concept: longest substring with at most k distinct chars
cue: Slide a window, shrinking left while the distinct-key count passes k.
prompt: Write `longestAtMostKDistinct(s, k)` that returns the length of the longest substring of `s` containing at most `k` distinct characters. Use a sliding window with a count-map.
mapsTo: LC 340 · Longest Substring with At Most K Distinct Characters

concept: fixed window: find all anagram start indices
cue: Slide a fixed p.length window and record where its char counts match p's.
prompt: Write `findAnagrams(s, p)` that returns an array of the start indices in `s` where an anagram of `p` begins, in increasing order. Slide a fixed window of length `p.length`.
mapsTo: LC 438 · Find All Anagrams in a String

concept: missing-count: how many chars still needed, with multiplicity
cue: missing starts at t.length; it drops by one only when the entering char was still needed (its need count was still positive before you decrement it).
note: One counter replaces re-scanning t on every step: missing === 0 means the window now covers t. Chars beyond what is needed push need below 0, so missing does not drop for them.

concept: minimum window covering all of t
cue: Expand right until t is fully covered, then shrink left to shorten while still covered.
prompt: Write `minWindow(s, t)` that returns the shortest substring of `s` containing every character of `t` including multiplicity, or `''` if none exists. Use a sliding window; keep each step O(1).
